title: Unnecessary Equality Checks Should Not Be Made
description: Comparisons of dissimilar types will always result in false, making the comparison and its dependent code redundant. This includes arrays, where calling equals on two arrays is the same as comparing their addresses. Some developers might expect Array.equals(Object obj) to compare the size and contents of the arrays, but it only does a simple memory address comparison. Instead, the == operator or Arrays.equals(array1, array2) should be used with arrays.
recommendation: Remove unnecessary equality checks. For arrays, use the == operator or Arrays.equals(array1, array2).
impact: Improves code readability and avoid unnecessary operations.

checks.message: Remove unnecessary equality checks. For arrays, use the == operator or Arrays.equals(array1, array2).
